_doc: Resume bullet library read by resume-tailoring (skill #13). This is the RICH schema the engine consumes. Each bullet has a stable BK-NNN id, the role tags it targets, competencies, whether it carries a quantified metric, and ATS keywords. role_profiles maps a role tag to its default tagline and about-me text: a per-application config picks a role tag, and the engine resolves the tagline and about-me through role_profiles, so the ids referenced here (tagline_<tag>, about_<tag>) must match the entries in taglines and about_me_versions. Fill every bracketed [placeholder] with your real content during onboarding and add as many bullets as you need (each gets the next free BK-NNN id and status "active"). resume-tailoring refuses to run while the bullets list is empty or any [bracket token] remains (the TD-051 placeholder guard). For a complete, filled, fictional worked example, see resume-tailoring/applications/_synthetic/career/bullet_library.json. No data here ever leaves your own PKS repo.

bullets.text: [One accomplishment, ideally with a number. Example: Cut order-processing time 35 percent by redesigning the fulfillment workflow.]

about_me_versions.text: [Two to four sentence professional summary for this role target. Lead with your years and scope, then what you own end to end. Leave a blank line to split it into two short paragraphs if you want.]

taglines.use_when: [When to reach for this tagline, e.g. general operations and program-management roles]

[role-tag, e.g. operations].description: [What kinds of roles this profile targets]
